text: 12. FINAL PAYMENT: Contractor shall make final payment to Subcontractor after the work is completed and accepted by Owner and/or  the  Project  Architect  or  Engineer,  providing  like  payment  shall  have  been  made  by  Owner  to  Contractor,  and  further providing that all other terms and conditions of payment have been satisfied, and Subcontractor shall have furnished Contractor with  a  waiver  and  release  of  lien  executed  by  Subcontractor,  and  waivers  and  release  of  liens  executed  by  Subcontractor's subcontractors and/or suppliers, or other satisfactory evidence that all labor and material accounts incurred by Subcontractor in connection  with  the  sublet  work  have  been  paid  in  full.  The  meaning  of  the  provisions  of  the  Prime  Contract,  as  it  may  be determined by any final judgment or award in or settlement of litigation or arbitration between Owner and Contractor will control with  respect  to  measurement,  quantities  and  payment,  including  payment  for  extras,  changes  and  variations  in  plans  and  size conditions. Upon receipt of final payment, Subcontractor agrees that neither he nor his subcontractors, suppliers or mechanics or anyone claiming under them shall have any lien upon the sublet work, the Project, or the improvements, property or premises on which  the  work  is  performed,  and  Subcontractor  hereby  waives  on  behalf  of  himself  and  his  subcontractors,  suppliers  and mechanics and those claiming under them, all right to any such lien. Subcontractor agrees to indemnify Contractor and Owner against all lien claims and bond claims, including expenses, costs of bonds to remove liens, and attorneys' fees related to such claims, which may be asserted by mechanics, materialmen, suppliers or subcontractors of Subcontractor or of his subcontractors and suppliers or anyone claiming under one of them. Subcontractor also agrees that if liens are placed on the work or the Project or the improvements, property or premises on which the work is performed, he will immediately obtain and record in the real property records of the county such bond which is necessary to remove such lien. If bond claims and/or lien claims are filed by any subcontractors, materialmen, suppliers or mechanics of Subcontractor, Contractor may suspend payments to Subcontractor and  either  hold  money  due  Subcontractor  or  make  payments  directly  to  the  claimants  and  charge  the  payments  against  sums allegedly due Subcontractor, without prejudice to any payment bond furnished by Subcontractor. It is the intent of the parties that any payment bond furnished by Subcontractor be for the benefit of Contractor and Owner, and not for the benefit of third parties except to the extent necessary to protect Contractor and Owner.
